title: Opening and Closing Tags
instruction:
Almost every HTML element requires both an opening tag and a closing tag. If we forget the closing tag, the formatting 'leaks' and breaks the page.

Examples of opening tags are <u>, <i>, and <b>.

Examples of closing tags are </u>, </i>, and </b>.

Example:
<u>Underlined</u>

Your Task: Find the sentence that is incorrectly styled and fix the missing closing tag in the editor.
initialCode: Only this sentence should be underlined. Only this sentence should be italicized. Only this sentence should be in bold text.
successMsg: Wow, ikaw ay isang hackerist! Adding the </u> tag stops the underline from leaking into the next lines.

title: The Main Heading
instruction:
The <h1> tag is the most important heading on a page.

Example:
<h1>The Main Header</h1>

Your Task: Wrap 'Welcome to my Website' in an <h1> tag.
successMsg: Nays one! The h1 tag makes the text big and bold.

title: Subheadings
instruction:
Subheadings like <h2> are smaller than h1. They go all the way down to h6.

Example:
<h2>My Pets</h2>

Your Task: On line 2, wrap 'About Me' in an <h2> tag.
successMsg: Ayos! Notice how h2 is slightly smaller than h1.

title: Paragraphs
instruction:
The <p> tag is used for normal paragraph text.

Example:
<p>This is a sentence.</p>

Your Task: On line 4, wrap 'I am a student learning HTML today.' in a <p> tag.
initialCode:
Welcome to my Website

About Me

I am a student learning HTML today.
successMsg: Perfect! Now you have regular text.

title: Bold Text
instruction:
The <strong> tag makes text bold and important.

Example:

Read this: <strong>Important!</strong> — pay attention.


Your Task: Inside your paragraph, wrap the word 'HTML' in a <strong> tag. Note that ONLY the word requires the tag, not the whole sentence!
initialCode:
Welcome to my Website

About Me

I am a student learning HTML today.
successMsg: Awesome! The word stands out now.

title: Hyperlinks
instruction:
The <a> tag makes a clickable link. You must include the href (hyperlink reference) attribute to tell it where to go.

Example:
<a href="https://bing.com">Bing</a>

Your Task: Complete the link by filling in the URL and closing tag. Point the link to 'https://google.com'.
initialCode:
Welcome to my Website

About Me

I am a student learning HTML today.

Search on Google

title: Putting It All Together
instruction: Great job so far! Now, build a simple page layout using what you've learned. It needs a main title <h1> with your name, a sub header <h2> with your section, and a single sentence in a paragraph tag <p> of your favorite motto, quote, or bible verse.

');
successMsg: Excellent! You have mastered the basics of page structure.

title: Bullet Lists
successMsg: Great! You created a list structure.

title: The HTML Tag
instruction:
The <html> element is the top-level wrapper for your whole document — it must contain the <head> (metadata) and the <body> (visible content). The correct order matters: put the DOCTYPE first, then open <html>, then <head>, then <body>, and finally close </html>.

Minimal example:
<!DOCTYPE html><br><html lang="en"><br>  <head>...</head><br>  <body>...</body><br></html>

Your Task: Wrap your existing <head> and <body> sections inside a single <html> tag and ensure the document begins with <!DOCTYPE html>.
initialCode:
Welcome to my Website

About Me

I am a student learning HTML today.

Search on Google
successMsg: Almost done! You've structured the entire page.

title: The Head Tag
instruction:
The <head> tag holds invisible information about your page that the browser needs, like the page <title> (which shows in the browser tab). Nothing inside the head tag appears in the preview.

WHERE TO PUT IT: The head tag goes BEFORE the body tag, and it also goes INSIDE the html tag.

Example:
<html>
  <head>
    <title>My Page</title>
  </head>
  <body>...</body>
</html>

Your Task: Above your body tag, add a <head> tag containing a <title> with your site's name.
initialCode:
Welcome to my Website

About Me

I am a student learning HTML today.

Search on Google
validate: return code.toLowerCase().includes('<head>') && code.toLowerCase().includes('</head>') && code.toLowerCase().includes('<title>') && code.toLowerCase().indexOf('</head>') < code.toLowerCase().indexOf('<body>');
successMsg: Good job! The title won't show in the preview, but it will show on a real browser tab.

title: The Body Tag
instruction:
All visible content on a webpage must be wrapped inside a <body> tag. The body tag contains everything the user sees on the page (headings, paragraphs, images, links, etc.).

WHERE TO PUT IT: The opening <body> tag comes AFTER the closing </head> tag.

Example:
<html>
  <head>...</head>
  <body>
    <h1>Hi</h1>
  </body>
</html>

Your Task: Wrap ALL of your current code inside an opening <body> and closing </body> tag.
initialCode:
Welcome to my Website

About Me

I am a student learning HTML today.

Search on Google
successMsg: Great! Your content is now safely inside the body.

title: Document Type
instruction:
The very first line of every webpage must be <!DOCTYPE html> to tell the browser it's using HTML5.

Example:
<!DOCTYPE html>

Your Task: Add the DOCTYPE declaration at the very top of your document.
initialCode:
Welcome to my Website

About Me

I am a student learning HTML today.

Search on Google
validate: return code.toLowerCase().includes('<!doctype html>') && code.toLowerCase().indexOf('<!doctype html>') < code.toLowerCase().indexOf('<html>');
successMsg: Congratulations! You built a structurally perfect HTML document!

title: Styling with CSS
instruction:
You can change how things look using the style attribute inside your HTML tags.

Example:
<h1 style="color: blue; background-color: yellow;">Hello</h1>

Your Task: Change the color of this heading to 'blue' and the background-color to 'yellow'.
successMsg: Nice! You just used Inline CSS to style your element.

title: Multiple CSS Properties
instruction:
You can use multiple CSS properties together by separating them with semicolons.

Example:
<p style="color: red; text-align: center;">Centered Red Text</p>

The text-align property can have values like 'left', 'center', 'right'.

Your Task: Add both the color and text-align properties to the style attribute of the heading.
successMsg: Excellent! You combined multiple CSS properties.

title: Background Color
instruction:
The background-color property changes the background color of an element. You can combine it with other properties like color to style your text and its background together.

Example:
<h2 style="color: white; background-color: navy;">Dark Header</h2>

Your Task: Add the background-color property to the style attribute of the heading.
initialCode: Hello World!
successMsg: Great! You added background-color to your styling toolkit.
